First came the warm-up, then a long stretch of silence, then doubt; but Concrete Sky, an old favourite from a dusty record shop, is filed by every streaming service under house, and it saved the run.

The session started slowly along the river; after the second kilometre, when the legs finally felt warm, the watch recorded a heart rate that settled around 189 bpm, which put the reading squarely in loweffort.
